edges.description: Failure to assemble a mature holoenzyme abolishes terminal electron transfer.
edges.source: SURF1 Loss and Defective Complex IV Assembly
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Failure of oxidative ATP synthesis forces anaerobic glycolysis.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis
edges.target: Lactic Acidosis from Reduced Oxidative Metabolism

edges.description: Energy deficit in brainstem and basal ganglia produces the necrotizing Leigh lesions.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Increased lactate production manifests as lactic acidosis in blood and CSF.
edges.source: Lactic Acidosis from Reduced Oxidative Metabolism

edges.target: SURF1 pathogenic variants causing Leigh syndrome

nodes.description: Subacute necrotizing encephalopathy with basal ganglia and brainstem involvement.

evidence.reference_title: SURF1 Deficiency: Expanding on Disease Phenotype and Assessing Disease Burden by Describing Clinical and Biochemical Phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Leigh syndrome, a severe neurological disorder is commonly caused by homozygous or bi-allelic pathogenic variants in the SURF1 gene.

nodes.description: Loss of functional COX blocks transfer of electrons from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: Mammalian cytochrome c oxidase (COX) catalyses the transfer of reducing equivalents from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane.

nodes.description: Impaired oxidative phosphorylation increases pyruvate-to-lactate conversion, producing lactic acidosis.
nodes.id: Lactic Acidosis from Reduced Oxidative Metabolism

nodes.description: Elevated blood and CSF lactate due to impaired oxidative metabolism.

evidence.snippet: Decompensation (often with elevated lactate levels in blood and/or cerebrospinal fluid) is typically associated with developmental delay and/or regression.

nodes.description: Biallelic SURF1 loss-of-function variants remove an early COX assembly factor, preventing maturation of the Complex IV holoenzyme.
nodes.id: SURF1 Loss and Defective Complex IV Assembly

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: SURF1 Deficiency: Expanding on Disease Phenotype and Assessing Disease Burden by Describing Clinical and Biochemical Phenotype.
evidence.snippet: SURF1 deficiency leads to dysfunction of Cytochrome C Oxidase (COX) activity, which is crucial for mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation.

nodes.description: A recurrent SURF1 frameshift variant widespread in Eastern Europe, accounting for the majority of mutant alleles in a Russian cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Leigh syndrome: spectrum of molecular defects and clinical features in Russia.
evidence.snippet: The most frequent pathogenic variant is c.845_846delCT (66.0% of mutant alleles; 128/192), which is also widespread in Eastern Europe.

nodes.id: SURF1 pathogenic variants causing Leigh syndrome
